name: agent-cf-implementer

scoring.0: No TDD — production code written without tests or tests added after the fact
scoring.1: Tests exist but were clearly written after production code (no failing phase visible)
scoring.2: RED-GREEN visible but REFACTOR step skipped or tests not re-run after changes
scoring.3: Full RED-GREEN-REFACTOR cycle: failing test shown, minimal code to pass, then clean refactor

criteria.name: code_quality
criteria.description: Production code is clean, follows project conventions, and is maintainable

scoring.0: Code has obvious bugs, poor naming, or violates project conventions
scoring.1: Code works but has readability issues or doesn't follow existing patterns
scoring.2: Code is clean and follows conventions but could be more idiomatic
scoring.3: Code is exemplary — follows project patterns, clean naming, proper error handling

criteria.name: test_quality
criteria.description: Tests are comprehensive, testing behavior over implementation

scoring.0: Tests are trivial or test implementation details (e.g., checking internal state)
scoring.1: Tests cover the happy path but miss error cases and edge cases
scoring.2: Good coverage of main scenarios but some edge cases untested
scoring.3: Comprehensive tests: happy path, edge cases, error handling, boundary conditions

criteria.name: minimal_changes
criteria.description: Changes are scoped to the task — no unrelated modifications

scoring.0: Significant unrelated changes mixed in (refactoring other files, adding unrelated features)
scoring.1: Some tangential changes that are not strictly required
scoring.2: Changes are well-scoped but include minor cleanup in touched files
scoring.3: Changes are precisely scoped to the task with no unrelated modifications

notes: Implementer should follow a plan if one exists. Multiple RED-GREEN-REFACTOR cycles are expected for complex features. Test files should be co-located with source files or in __tests__/ directories per project convention.
